justification: All pass criteria met: 9 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1), shipped/delivered rejection (AC-2), already-cancelled idempotency (AC-3), plus 6 additional scenarios. At least 2 ambiguities surfaced — low_confidence_areas lists 6 (cancellable statuses, partial cancellation, refund method, reason required/optional, time-based window, coupon policy) and 3 formal escalations (cancellation window, partial cancellation, refund policy). confidence.level=45 is well under the 65 threshold. No business rules are invented as facts — assumptions are clearly labeled, AC-5 explicitly defers Processing-stage behavior to escalation, and BR-4 (authorization) is a reasonable security invariant rather than an invented domain rule. edge_cases section is populated with 8 detailed scenarios.

observations.text: 9 acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum 3, covering happy path, two required edge cases, plus authorization, inventory, refund, retry, and atomicity scenarios
confidence.level=45 accurately reflects the underspecified input — well-calibrated
3 structured escalations with options and recommendations properly flag cancellation window, partial cancellation, and refund policy as ambiguities requiring stakeholder input
low_confidence_areas explicitly calls out 'Exact set of cancellable statuses', 'Time-based cancellation window', and 'Partial cancellation' — directly matching expected ambiguities
AC-4 and BR-4 address the 'who can cancel' question, and the edge_cases include admin/agent cancellation on behalf of user
Edge cases are exceptionally thorough — concurrent requests, payment gateway failures, promotional discount restoration, auth hold vs captured payment
AC-5 explicitly refuses to invent an answer for Processing status, deferring to escalation instead of assuming

Minor: BR-4 presents authorization as an invariant without noting the PM never specified who can cancel — though it is flagged in edge_cases (admin scenario), it could be more explicitly called out as an assumption in low_confidence_areas

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 9 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1), shipped/delivered rejection (AC-2), already-cancelled terminal state (AC-4), plus additional edge cases. Multiple ambiguities surfaced in low_confidence_areas (cancellable states, cancellation window, partial cancellation, who can cancel via BR-5/AC-5) and escalations. Confidence level 45 is well below 65 threshold. No invented business rules presented as facts — assumptions are clearly labeled and escalations offer options rather than dictating answers.

observations.text: 9 acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum 3, covering happy path, shipped/delivered, already-cancelled, authorization, partial fulfillment, payment failures, and promotions
6 low_confidence_areas explicitly surfaced including cancellable states, cancellation window, partial cancellation, subscriptions, and loyalty points
Confidence level 45 appropriately reflects the underspecified single-sentence input
3 structured escalations with options and recommendations rather than invented answers
Edge cases section is comprehensive with 8 scenarios including race conditions, idempotency, and payment edge cases
Authorization question addressed directly in BR-5 and AC-5 — who can cancel is explicitly covered
Assumptions are clearly labeled in the confidence section rather than presented as facts

Escalation 1 recommendation suggests cancellation up to Processing state — while framed as a recommendation with alternatives, this borders on inventing a business rule. However, it is clearly labeled as a recommendation among options, not a fact.

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1), shipped (AC-2), delivered (AC-3), already-cancelled (AC-4), unauthorized user (AC-5), and more. At least 5 explicit ambiguities surfaced in low_confidence_areas and escalations (cancellation window, partial cancellation, promotion handling, non-cancellable item types, refund calculation). Confidence level 45 is well below the 65 threshold. Edge cases section is extensively populated with 9 scenarios. Business rules flagged as assumptions where appropriate (BR-5, BR-6 marked non-invariant). No invented rules presented as facts — ambiguous boundaries like Processing state explicitly flagged in AC-6 and escalations.

observations.text: 10 acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum 3, covering happy path, shipped, delivered, already-cancelled, unauthorized user, processing ambiguity, multi-item, audit, refund failure, and concurrency
Confidence level of 45 correctly reflects the underspecified input, well below the 65 threshold
5 low_confidence_areas explicitly surfaced: cancellable states, partial cancellation, promotion handling, non-cancellable items, refund calculation
3 detailed escalations with options and recommendations for cancellation window, partial cancellation, and refund/promotion policy
9 edge cases covering race conditions, concurrent requests, subscription orders, promotion codes, and payment authorization holds
Who-can-cancel question addressed directly in BR-2 and AC-5, and flagged as an invariant rather than silently assumed

BR-2 presents 'only the order owner can cancel' as an invariant fact rather than flagging it as an assumption — the PM did not specify who can cancel, and operations staff cancellation is a legitimate possibility that should be surfaced as an ambiguity
Cancellation Window defined in ubiquitous_language as if it is an established domain term, though the PM never mentioned it — minor overreach but flagged appropriately in escalations
